meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale Projects
content.seo_description: Stop manual coding. Use this SaaS starter kit framework with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance projects and boost profitability today.
content.answer_block: To scale freelance projects, stop building from scratch. Implement a modular SaaS starter kit to automate auth, payments, and database schemas. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you reduce development time by 70%, allowing you to ship production-ready SaaS apps at lightning speed.
content.intro:
The late-night burnout is a universal freelancer experience. You’re trapped in a cycle of 'starting from zero' for every new client, wrestling with boilerplate configuration, and manually wiring up authentication flows. Your growth is capped by the speed of your fingers, and your margins are eroding under the weight of repetitive, low-value setup tasks.

But the best engineers don't build—they assemble. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from artisan hand-crafting to industrial-grade assembly. This is how you shift from a stressed freelancer to a scalable product studio, reclaiming your time while increasing your delivery velocity.

steps.title: Define the Universal Architecture
steps.description: Establish a base repository containing your preferred tech stack (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe). Use Antigravity to generate the structural blueprint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade SaaS starter kit architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase Auth, and Stripe webhooks. Include a modular directory structure for features, utilities, and UI components.

steps.title: Automate Authentication Logic
steps.description: Don't write auth from scratch. Use Claude Code to inject your pre-tested authentication modules into the new project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Supabase authentication wrapper that handles user sessions, protected routes, and middleware redirects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate auth-wrapper.ts into the project and configure middleware.ts for private routes'

steps.title: Standardize Database Schemas
steps.description: Use a reusable migration set. Ensure every client project shares the same database foundation to prevent data drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft SQL migration files for a standard SaaS: users, subscriptions, and organizations tables with RLS policies enabled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply migration files and verify RLS policies in the database schema'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Hooks
steps.description: Standardize your payment gateway integration so you don't have to debug webhooks for every single client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler that maps subscription events to local database status updates for active users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe-webhook-handler.ts and configure the endpoint route'

steps.title: Finalize and Deploy
steps.description: Automate the CI/CD pipeline setup to ensure your starter kit is ready for production deployment on Vercel or Railway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github-actions for deployment and run a final project audit'

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product, not a folder; version it on GitHub and use tags to manage updates.
Always include a 'feature-flag' system in your base kit to toggle paid features for different clients.
Use Claude Code to scan for security vulnerabilities in your starter kit once a month to keep your base clean.
Automate your environment variable injection using a standard .env.example template to avoid configuration headaches.
Keep your UI components in a separate private npm package to update the design of all your client projects simultaneously.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is stabilized, create a 'Client Onboarding' document that maps your specific project folder structure so you can hand off projects to contractors without losing quality.
content.conversion_block: Stop trading your hours for fixed-fee misery. Build a system that scales so you can focus on high-ticket architecture rather than boilerplate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
